message:
feat: Implement rs-bpe backtracking encoder (INCOMPLETE)

Port Status:
✅ Aho-Corasick (400 lines from daachorse)
✅ split_table + isValidPair validation
✅ cl100k_base regex splitter
✅ BacktrackEncoder struct with step() logic
❌ WRONG RESULTS: greedy matching doesn't work for merge-based BPE

Implementation:
- BacktrackEncoder.init() uses Aho-Corasick for longest-match
- step() method with backtracking + bitfield
- isValidPair() stub (returns true for all pairs)
- nextPrefix() for trying shorter tokens

Current State:
- Correctness: WRONG ([68, 68, 75...] vs [9906, 1917])
- Speed: 33x slower (14.7s vs 0.45s for 100 iterations)

Root Issue:
tiktoken/cl100k_base uses MERGE-BASED BPE:
- Apply merges by priority/rank order
- NOT greedy longest-match
- Backtracking with longest-match is fundamentally wrong approach

Would need to rewrite to use priority queue for merge-based encoding
